title: horkelia pygmaea
intro: Horkelia pygmaea, also known as dwarf horkelia, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its rich composition of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Horkelia pygmaea have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Horkelia pygmaea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
